title: Maps: a single sheet vs. a map series
audience: Map libraries, cadastral and survey archives
intro: Maps come as single sheets or as series, and they are always about a place. This scenario branches on sheet-vs-series, attaches the surveyor or cartographer, and — the move people most often miss — links the map to the area it covers as a first-class Place.
question: Is a map sheet a Record and a series a Record Set? How do I record the cartographer and the area covered?

steps.prompt: One map sheet, or a whole series?

choices.label: A single sheet
choices.why: Good — one sheet is one Record.

choices.label: A map series
choices.why: Good — a series (e.g. all sheets of a survey) is a Record Set, with each sheet a member Record.

choices.label: A Place
choices.why: Not the map itself. The area a map depicts is a Place you relate to it — but the map sheet/series is a Record/Record Set.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. The sheet is one Record. Scale and projection are its attributes; the printed sheet is an Instantiation; the area is a related Place.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one sheet — a Set is for the series.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: That's the physical printed sheet. The map's content is the Record; the paper is an Instantiation of it.

capture.comment: Create the map sheet as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. The series is an aggregation of sheets — a Record Set — and each sheet is a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a series is many sheets, each with its own coverage and edition. Use a Record Set of sheet Records.

choices.label: Activity
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The survey that produced the series could be an Activity, but the series itself is the aggregated records.

capture.comment: Create the series as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: Attach the surveyor / cartographer (a person or a survey body).

choices.label: Agent + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The surveyor or mapping agency is an Agent (a Person or a Corporate Body). Relate the map to them with has_creator; the same agency Agent is reused across every sheet it produced.

choices.label: A note in the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit — agencies and surveyors should be reconcilable Agents, not free text, so their whole output can be found together.

capture.comment: Create the cartographer / survey body as an Agent.
Link the map to its maker with has_creator.

steps.prompt: The area the map covers — how do you record it?

choices.label: Place + has_or_had_location
choices.why: Fits. The covered area is a Place entity, related to the map with has_or_had_location. As a first-class Place it can be reconciled (gazetteer, coordinates) and shared across every map and record about that area.

choices.label: Type the place name into the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit. A place buried in a title can't be searched spatially or linked to other material about the same place. Model it as a Place.

choices.label: A Record
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The area is a location, not a record. Use a Place.

capture.comment: Create the covered area as a Place.
Link the map to the place with has_or_had_location.

outcome.verdict: Map, maker and place — all linked.
outcome.summary: The sheet is a Record (or the series a Record Set); the surveyor is a reusable Agent; and the covered area is a first-class Place. That last link is what turns a map catalogue into something you can search by place — and connect to every other record about the same area.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
